name: Welsh Tea Cakes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine the dry ingredients and blend well.
2. Using a pastry blender, cut in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Stir in the currants (or raisins).
3. Place the egg in a 1-cup measure and beat lightly with a fork.
4. Add milk to fill the 1/4 cup mark; pour into the flour mixture and stir with a fork to form a soft dough. A little more milk may be needed.
5. On a floured surface, shape the dough into a ball and knead lightly 5-6 times.
6. Roll the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch. Using a floured small biscuit cutter, cut into rounds.
7. Bake on a cookie sheet at 350 degrees F until slightly browned.
8. Cool on a rack.
9. Store in a metal container.
